source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A hexagon is drawn with its vertices at $$(0,0),(1,0),(2,1),(2,2),(1,2), \text{ and } (0,1),$$ and all of its diagonals are also drawn, as shown below. The diagonals cut the hexagon into $24$ regions of various shapes and sizes. These $24$ regions are shown in pink and yellow below. If the smallest region (by area) has area $a$, and the largest has area $b$, then what is the ratio $a:b$? Give your answer in lowest terms. <image1>
